# 07 Project DentOS User Acceptance Criteria

## Release Standard

The product is accepted only when:

1. Every `PRODUCT_CONTRACT` screen, command, and analytic in documents 01-06 maps to an implemented route, control, database transition, permission, audit event, and test.
2. All critical tests below pass against one repeatable synthetic clinic fixture.
3. UI grid totals, exports, source transactions, cached balances, and journal postings reconcile.
4. No open Severity 1 or 2 defect remains.
5. Items marked `OPTIONAL_DESIGN` remain disabled until they pass their named feature-flag tests in a non-production DentOS clinic.

Passing this suite establishes compliance with the Project DentOS product, security, clinical, accounting, and operational contracts.

## Release Blockers — Unresolved Financial Logic

The six decisions below are hard release blockers for their dependent financial paths. A passing test is necessary but not sufficient: finance, product, architecture, and quality must approve the observed result in a versioned decision record before the corresponding `UNRESOLVED-xx` marker may be removed or its Phase 2 feature enabled.

| Blocker | Decision | Resolving test | Release condition |
|---|---|---|---|
| `UNRESOLVED-01` | Whether and how a new Collection is automatically allocated, including eligible Fee Statements, order, partial allocation, and retries | `FIN-DEC-01` | selected policy passes idempotency, concurrency, audit, balance, and unsettled-register assertions |
| `UNRESOLVED-02` | Number-series scope, clinic-timezone period boundary, rollover, reset, and issued/reserved number reuse | `FIN-DEC-02` | selected series policy passes boundary, rollback, void, collision, and concurrent-allocation assertions |
| `UNRESOLVED-03` | Open Fee Exposure age anchor, as-of event treatment, and exact bucket edges | `FIN-DEC-03` | source SQL, grid, totals, drill-down, print, and export agree on every boundary row |
| `UNRESOLVED-04` | Multi-clinician Fee Allocation distribution and doctor/service-domain attribution | `FIN-DEC-04` | distribution cells reconcile to application, line, tender, clinician, category, and reversal totals at INR 0.00 variance |
| `UNRESOLVED-05` | Refund eligibility and settlement handling when the source Collection is already allocated | `FIN-DEC-05` | selected policy preserves balanced journals, dated effects, immutable history, and non-negative source balances |
| `UNRESOLVED-06` | One-method Collection entries versus split-tender Collections and downstream mode lineage | `FIN-DEC-06` | entry, allocation, refund, cashier pivot, method pivot, and export reconcile without row multiplication |

### FIN-DEC-01 [Phase 2] Automatic Fee Allocation Policy

1. Create three issued Fee Statements for Patient A with outstanding values `300.00`, `500.00`, and `900.00`; use distinct issue dates and leave all three open.
2. Post one `1,000.00` Collection with automatic allocation disabled; verify no `fee_allocations`, distribution rows, receivable transfers, or treatment-progression events exist and the full `1,000.00` appears in the unsettled register.
3. Enable only the candidate policy named in the versioned decision record, post an equivalent Collection, and capture the eligible Fee Statements, ordering key, partial-allocation rule, and policy version written to audit.
4. Submit the same command twice with one idempotency key and concurrently with two cashiers; verify one Collection and no allocation amount exceeding Collection availability or Fee Statement due.
5. Recalculate collection gross, unapplied value, Fee Statement due, journal debits/credits, clinician distributions, and unsettled-register rows from source records.
6. Require an approved decision record stating whether automation is disabled or enabled and, when enabled, its exact eligibility, sort order, partial handling, checkout behavior, and retry semantics.

Expected: the selected policy is deterministic, idempotent, auditable, balanced to INR `0.00`, and produces no guessed allocation path; otherwise `UNRESOLVED-01` remains open.

### FIN-DEC-02 [Phase 2] Document Number Scope and Rollover

1. Configure the candidate numbering modes for Patient Code, Case Number, Statement Reference, Collection Reference, Refund Number, Laboratory Order, Expense Voucher, Goods In, and Goods Out in Central and North clinics.
2. Allocate the last number before and first number after a clinic-local month boundary, calendar-year boundary, and approved fiscal boundary; verify the period key and counter selected by the candidate policy.
3. Roll back one transaction after reservation, void one issued document, and reset a permitted future counter; verify no reserved or issued value is reused.
4. Submit 20 concurrent allocations for one clinic/type/period and verify unique monotonic values under the selected format with one reservation row per result.
5. Attempt a manual collision in each clinic and verify `409`, no source document, no counter corruption, and a denial/collision audit record.
6. Require an approved decision record naming scope, timezone, period calendar, format, reset permissions, rollback-gap treatment, void treatment, and migration behavior.

Expected: uniqueness and non-reuse hold across every boundary and concurrency case; otherwise `UNRESOLVED-02` remains open.

### FIN-DEC-03 [Phase 2] Open Fee Exposure Aging Policy

1. Set one As-of Date and create open Fee Statements whose candidate age-start dates produce age days `0`, `30`, `31`, `60`, `61`, `90`, and `91`; include one row with a due date and one with no due date.
2. Add applications, credits, reliefs, and reversals immediately before, on, and after the As-of Date.
3. Execute Due Fee Statements and every patient-balance/clinician-split aging variant with clinic, statement-date, patient-category, clinician, minimum-due, and bucket filters.
4. Recalculate due from dated source events and assign each positive-due Fee Statement to exactly one candidate bucket.
5. Compare source SQL, grid rows, footer amounts/counts, drill-down, print, PDF, and spreadsheet output.
6. Require an approved decision record naming the age anchor, inclusivity of every bucket edge, as-of reversal rule, void-effective rule, and timezone interpretation.

Expected: each row appears once, all output formats agree, and variance is INR `0.00`; otherwise `UNRESOLVED-03` remains open.

### FIN-DEC-04 [Phase 2] Multi-Clinician Applied-Collection Attribution

1. Issue one Fee Statement with a `600.00` Endodontics line owned by clinician A and a `400.00` Implantology line owned by clinician B.
2. Apply `500.00` from one Collection and execute the candidate allocation policy; under the documented provisional rule verify `300.00` for clinician A and `200.00` for clinician B.
3. Repeat with three unequal line balances and an amount that produces residual cents; verify deterministic largest-remainder ordering by fractional remainder, line sequence, and line ID when that policy is selected.
4. Reconcile application header, tender splits, Fee Statement-line splits, tender-by-line distribution, clinician totals, service-domain totals, and clinician-share basis.
5. Reverse the application and verify immutable distribution history remains while current applied-collection reports exclude the reversed amount.
6. Require an approved decision record naming explicit versus proportional allocation, residual-cent ordering, snapshot columns, manual override permission, and reversal treatment.

Expected: every marginal and matrix sum equals the application and all current reports reverse cleanly; otherwise `UNRESOLVED-04` remains open.

### FIN-DEC-05 [Phase 2] Refund of an Allocated Collection

1. Post a `1,000.00` Collection, allocate `700.00` to a Fee Statement, and attempt a `400.00` refund without first changing the allocation.
2. Verify the candidate policy either rejects the refund without any write or performs only the specifically approved atomic deallocation/adjustment path; no implicit behavior is accepted.
3. Complete the permitted sequence, then verify Collection available/unapplied, Fee Statement due, refund tender lineage, patient advance/receivable, and all journal lines.
4. Reverse the refund on a later date and verify the original refund remains immutable, the reversal has its own dated effect, and gross Collection on the original date is unchanged.
5. Compare gross, refund, net collection, open exposure, unsettled value, cashier, and method reports before refund, after refund, and after reversal.
6. Require an approved decision record naming refund eligibility, deallocation order, negative-receivable support, tender selection, approval threshold, and accounting-date rule.

Expected: no source balance becomes negative, every journal balances, and dated reports reconcile to INR `0.00`; otherwise `UNRESOLVED-05` remains open.

### FIN-DEC-06 [Phase 2] Collection Method and Split-Tender Model

1. Record a `400.00` Cash plus `600.00` UPI payment using the candidate one-entry or two-entry model and retain the exact tender lineage.
2. Apply `700.00` to a Fee Statement; verify Cash and UPI application amounts according to the selected tender-allocation policy and verify their sum is `700.00`.
3. Leave `300.00` unapplied, refund `200.00` through the permitted source tender, and verify remaining tender balances never become negative.
4. Run date-wise gross Collection, collection-method pivot, cashier pivot, applied-collection by method, unsettled Collection, refund, and net-collection reports.
5. Compare grid, drill-down, print, PDF, and spreadsheet totals and verify no join multiplies the `700.00` application across both methods.
6. Require an approved decision record naming entry model, tender-allocation policy, UI fields, refund tender rule, historical migration, and feature-flag behavior.

Expected: gross, applied, unapplied, refunded, and net totals reconcile at both receipt and tender grain; otherwise `UNRESOLVED-06` remains open.

## Phase 1 Acceptance Group

Phase 1 is independently releasable when every heading tagged `[Phase 1]` passes and no Phase 1 Severity 1 or 2 defect remains. Phase 2 and Phase 3 failures do not fail this gate because their routes, commands, workers, and controls remain disabled.

| Phase 1 test family | Included contracts |
|---|---|
| Shell and MVP workflow | `UI-*`; `PAT-001` through `PAT-005`; `SCH-*`; `DSH-*`; `LED-001`; `SET-003`; `IAM-*`; `SEC-*`; `AUD-*`; `REL-*`; `PERF-*` when tagged `[Phase 1]` |
| Atomic contracts | every `CTL-*`, `PERM-*`, and `JOB-*` heading tagged `[Phase 1]` |
| Core reports | `RPT-001 Priority Views / Patient Encounters Register`, `RPT-066 Patient Intelligence / Daily Register Patients`, and `RPT-084 Booking Operations / Care Booking List` |
| Phase boundary | every control tagged `[Phase 2]` or `[Phase 3]` is unavailable by route guard, feature flag, permission decision, or disabled state and cannot mutate data through a direct API request |

## Phase 2 Acceptance Group

Phase 2 cannot begin its production release gate until all six `FIN-DEC-*` tests pass and all six `UNRESOLVED-xx` decision records are approved. It then requires every heading tagged `[Phase 2]`, including full financial posting, settlement, numbering, refund, aging, attribution, export, reconciliation, and every report leaf not named in Phase 1.

| Phase 2 test family | Included contracts |
|---|---|
| Decision closure | `FIN-DEC-01` through `FIN-DEC-06` and the Release Blockers table above |
| Ledger completion | `LED-002` through `LED-012`, `SER-*`, financial `REP-*`, and every Phase 2 permission/control/worker contract |
| Report completion | every `RPT-*` heading tagged `[Phase 2]`, plus grid/drill-down/print/PDF/spreadsheet equality and denied-export checks |
| Phase boundary | every `[Phase 3]` route, command, worker, and control remains disabled and produces no domain mutation |

## Phase 3 Acceptance Group

Phase 3 requires every heading tagged `[Phase 3]` after Phase 1 remains green and approved Phase 2 financial behavior continues to reconcile.

| Phase 3 test family | Included contracts |
|---|---|
| Advanced clinical | `CLN-*`, `REC-*`, `RX-*`, `INT-*`, `CAS-*`, `PAY-*`, and their Phase 3 control/permission/worker contracts |
| Extended operations | `COM-*`, `OFF-*`, `INV-*`, `LAB-*`, patient merge/full workspace, patient files, education, backup/restore, and document output |
| Administration | complete multi-clinic Practice Identity, advanced System Configuration, communications providers, medication masters, assets, and governance surfaces |
| Regression | all Phase 1 and Phase 2 tests rerun against the Phase 3 migration and enabled feature set |

## 1. Test Fixture

Create only synthetic data:

```text
Organization: Demo Dental Group
Clinics: Central, North
Users: admin, receptionist, cashier A, cashier B, clinician A, clinician B, read-only auditor
Roles: Security Admin, Reception, Cashier, Clinician, Auditor
Chairs: Chair 1, Chair 2, Chair 3
Collection modes: Cash, UPI, Card, Net Banking, Cheque
Fee schedules: Standard, Corporate
Patients: Register Patient A, Established Patient B, Family Patient C
Services: Consultation 500, RCT 5000, Crown 6000
Tax variants: non-taxable and configured GST example
```

Reset fixture between test runs or record the exact starting snapshot. Never use real patient names, phones, medical data, or financial details.

## 2. Evidence Required Per Test

For each test record:

- Test ID, build, date, tester, clinic, role
- Preconditions and fixture IDs
- Screenshot or screen recording with synthetic data
- API request/response ID or audit correlation ID
- Relevant source rows and status history
- Report/export totals when financial
- Pass/fail and defect link

## 3. Navigation and Visual Density

### UI-001 [Phase 1] Primary navigation order

- Sign in as admin at 1440x900.
- Verify first-level order is `Dashboard, Clinical Queue, Scheduler, Patient Registry, Practice Assets, Comms Center, Financial Operations, Deep Analytics, System Configuration`.
- Verify each label, position, route, and permission-gated visibility matches document 06.
- Verify active route is visibly selected and browser back/forward preserves route.
- Verify active clinic and reference date remain visible.

Expected: all nine routes open without full-page logout/reload; utility actions remain in Account menu.

### UI-002 [Phase 1] Dense shell dimensions

- Measure global navigation, toolbar, input, grid header, and standard row on Dashboard, Patient Registry, Clinical Queue, and Deep Analytics.
- Verify dimensions match `06_ui_and_menu_hierarchy.md` within 2 px.
- Verify no page uses large marketing cards, hero headings, decorative gradient/orbs, or excessive empty margins.
- Verify primary actions and first 15-20 normal grid rows are visible at 1440x900 where fixture data permits.

### UI-003 [Phase 1] Minimum laptop width

- Run at 1024x768.
- Open all nine routes and key modals.
- Verify no text/control overlap, no clipped Save/Cancel, and no hidden Clinical Queue row actions.
- Verify data grids/resource schedules use controlled horizontal scrolling instead of card conversion.

### UI-004 [Phase 1] Keyboard operation

- On Patient Registry press `/`; verify primary search receives focus.
- Use arrows/Tab/Shift+Tab across grids and forms.
- Press Enter on a selected row/action.
- Press Escape in clean and dirty modals.
- Use `Alt+N` on Patient Registry, Scheduler, Clinical Queue, and Practice Assets.
- Submit a valid non-destructive form with `Ctrl+Enter`.

Expected: focus is visible, logical, restored after close, and no shortcut fires while typing incompatible text.

## 4. Patient Lifecycle

### PAT-001 [Phase 1] New patient registration

1. Open `Patient Registry`, then select `Register Patient`.
2. Complete row 1 in this order: `Mobile Number`, `Given Name`, `Family Name`; verify `Patient ID` is read-only.
3. Complete row 2: `Preferred Name`, `Honorific`, `Birth Date`, `Gender Identity`, `Lead Clinician`.
4. Complete row 3: `Email`, `Alternate Phone`, `Communication Language`; enable SMS in `Welcome Consent` and leave WhatsApp disabled.
5. Complete row 4: `Postal Code`, `City`, `Locality`, `Street Address`.
6. Complete row 5 using a synthetic `Identity Type` and `Identity Number`, then set `Acquisition Channel` and `Referred By`.
7. Complete row 6: `Patient Segment`, `Fee Profile`, `Occupation`, `Emergency Contact`.
8. Enter `Registration Notes`, select `Save Profile`, then immediately replay the same request with the same idempotency key.

Expected:

- Exactly one patient and patient serial are created.
- Full KYC value is absent from list, logs, and audit JSON; masked value appears only to permitted role.
- One welcome SMS is queued after commit; no WhatsApp is queued.
- Patient is searchable by name, patient code, normalized mobile, and email.

### PAT-002 [Phase 1] Duplicate warning and override

1. Attempt another patient with same normalized mobile/email.
2. Verify candidate warning displays patient code/name without exposing unauthorized fields.
3. Cancel; verify no row/serial consumed as used.
4. Retry as authorized user and override with reason.

Expected: override is audited; accidental duplicate by receptionist is blocked according to clinic policy.

### PAT-003 [Phase 1] Search filters

- Test `Location`, `Birth Window`, `Household`, `Acquisition Channel`, `Referring Patient`, `Open Balance Above`, `Custom Attributes`, `Continuity Due`, and `Encounter Date` independently and in combinations.
- Compare total count to direct fixture expectations.
- Change filter while on page 2.

Expected: AND semantics, page resets to 1, stable sorting and paging, and `Encounter Date` uses committed care encounters.

### PAT-004 [Phase 1] Booking and encounter handoff from a saved patient

- Save with `Save and Create Booking`; verify the patient is preselected and no duplicate demographic row is created.
- Repeat with `Save and Start Encounter`; verify the patient is selected and one unscheduled Clinical Queue encounter is created for the chosen clinic and operational date.
- Verify `last_encounter_date` changes only for the encounter, never for care booking creation.

### PAT-005 [Phase 1] Medical history and allergy warning

- Add a severe synthetic allergy and medical answers.
- Engage an encounter and open Medication Orders and Delivered Care.
- Verify prominent, non-color-only warning.
- Attempt access as receptionist/read-only auditor.

Expected: role restrictions apply; all changes have history; inactive allergy master remains visible historically.

### PAT-006 [Phase 3] Merge

- Create a duplicate containing a care booking, unallocated collection, patient flag, and custom attribute.
- Preview merge map.
- Merge into survivor as authorized admin.

Expected: all links move once, collection receipt/application totals remain unchanged, duplicate searches redirect/show merged status, patient numbers remain auditable, and undo is not presented as a casual action.

### PAT-007 [Phase 3] Patient workspace and patient reports

1. Open a synthetic patient and verify this exact tab order: `Care Overview, Activity Stream, Odontogram, Diagnostics, Care Plan, Delivered Care, Clinical Notes, Medication Orders, Fee Statements, Collections, Lab Cases, Files, Communications`.
2. From Care Overview test `Edit Profile`, `Start Encounter`, `Create Booking`, and `Add Continuity Task`.
3. Open `Clinical Summary`, `Account Ledger`, `Fee Detail`, `Statement Ledger`, `Activity Timeline`, and `Patient Card`; set the date range where supported and close without generating unnecessary files.
4. Move among all tabs, use browser back/forward, then return to the registry.

Expected: patient context never changes accidentally; tab URLs are deep-linkable; registry page/filter/selection is restored; report date range affects only the selected patient/analytics.

## 5. Scheduler and Clinical Queue Handoff

### SCH-001 [Phase 1] View consistency

1. Create one care booking with clinician, chair, reason, duration, and notifications.
2. Locate the same care booking in Month, Week, Day, Resource Day, and Resource Week.
3. Switch views repeatedly, navigate dates, refresh, and print.

Expected: one care booking ID, same time/status/resources in all views; selected date/filter persists; print matches visible scope.

### SCH-002 [Phase 1] Chair conflict

- Create Care Booking A in Chair 1 from 10:00-10:30.
- Attempt Care Booking B in Chair 1 from 10:15-10:45.
- Attempt same save simultaneously from two browser sessions.

Expected: only one overlapping active care booking can commit. Conflict identifies chair/time. Authorized override, if enabled, requires permission and reason and is visibly marked.

### SCH-003 [Phase 1] Clinician conflict and different chair

- Schedule clinician A in Chair 1.
- Attempt overlapping clinician A in Chair 2.
- Repeat under policy allowing/disallowing clinician overlap.

Expected: configured policy is applied transactionally; no race-created double booking.

### SCH-004 [Phase 1] Reserve Resource Time

- Create clinician-only, chair-only, and clinician+chair blocks.
- Verify each appears in applicable views and is excluded from slot availability.
- Try block over a care booking and test policy/override.
- Cancel block and verify availability returns with audit retained.

### SCH-005 [Phase 1] New vs Established care booking

- Create a New patient care booking using snapshot details.
- Create an Established care booking by selecting patient B.
- Edit/reschedule/cancel/no-show each as applicable.

Expected: New snapshot does not become a patient until configured save/arrival flow; established link remains stable; status history records every transition.

### DSH-001 [Phase 1] Care Booking check-in creates one encounter

1. Open Clinical Queue on scheduled care date.
2. Click CheckIn twice rapidly and from a second session.
3. Refresh/filter by clinician/type/patient.

Expected:

- Exactly one encounter links to care_booking.
- Care Booking becomes arrived; encounter becomes checked-in.
- One queue sequence is consumed; timestamps are not overwritten by retry.
- Dashboard/Scheduler/Clinical Queue show consistent status.

### DSH-002 [Phase 1] Walk-in branches

- Create Established walk-in for patient B.
- Create New walk-in with synthetic demographics.
- Create encounter through `Patient` action from patient C.

Expected: three encounters with correct type; established/direct branches create no duplicate patient; new branch creates exactly one patient according to configured transaction.

### DSH-003 [Phase 1] State/action matrix

- For each encounter state verify only actions specified in `06` are visible.
- Transition waiting -> check-in -> engage -> checkout.
- Test permitted direct engage.
- Attempt invalid transition by API and UI.

Expected: invalid transition returns conflict/validation, not a silent state change; each valid transition has actor/time/history.

### DSH-004 [Phase 1] Concurrent queue edit

- User A opens Edit while User B engages the same care_encounter.
- User A attempts stale save.

Expected: row-version conflict; no status rollback; user can reload and reapply safe changes.

### DSH-005 [Phase 1] Checkout without fee statement

- Complete checkout with chargeable service but no fee_statement.
- Test as normal and override roles.

Expected: clinic policy blocks or requires explicit reason/permission. Encounter state, unassessed production candidate, and audit remain consistent.

## 6. Treatment, Recall, and Medication Order

### CLN-001 [Phase 3] Conformance plan and multi-tooth entries

- Select `Create Care Plan` and verify `Plan`, `Proposed On`, `By`, `Amount`, `Status`, Search, Category, and Comments.
- Add at least three items across different teeth/treatment areas/materials, including one service created through `Add Service Definition`.
- Verify on-the-fly fields and flags match document 06; save and reopen the care_plan.

Expected: displayed Amount equals the saved item total; tooth/material/fee/tax snapshots persist; no Fee Statement or Collection is created implicitly. Phase and item-acceptance controls do not appear in DentOS core mode because they were not specified.

Optional extension test: when phased planning is explicitly enabled, create two phases, partially accept items, and verify phase totals/history without changing the default conformance form.

### CLN-002 [Phase 3] Plan version/fee change

- Change fee schedule/master fee after proposal.
- Reopen plan and attempt edit.

Expected: original proposal snapshot remains; new version or explicit adjustment is created; historical reports do not change silently.

### REC-001 [Phase 3] Checkout recall creation

- Configure six-month recall rule for completed service.
- Complete encounter twice/retry checkout.

Expected: one recall only; due date uses actual completion date and clinic timezone.

### REC-002 [Phase 3] Recall queue lifecycle

- Run due worker, mark contacted, snooze, return to due, book care booking, complete linked care_encounter.

Expected: status history is complete; completed recall disappears from open queue; next interval is generated once.

### RX-001 [Phase 3] Medication Order save

- Create a medication order from the Medications or Templates stage.
- Verify `Save as Protocol`, `Save`, `Prescribing Clinician`, `Date`, Search, Category, and Clinical Guidance.
- Add a medication and populate `Strength`, `Take`, `Frequency`, `For` duration/value, and `Instructions`; save and reopen.

Expected: one medication order is saved with the prescribing-clinician/date snapshot and medication instructions. A separate `Sign` action is absent in DentOS core mode. If digital signing is enabled as an extension, its authorization, freeze, and superseding-flow tests become mandatory.

### RX-002 [Phase 3] Allergy and dose guard

- Select medication containing an active ingredient that matches a severe allergy.
- Enter weight-based dose above configured maximum.

Expected: warning/block follows configured extension policy and requires clinician acknowledgement where permitted; calculation shows units and maximum; audit records override. Automated dose calculation was not confirmed in the live conformance screen and must remain feature-flagged until separately validated.

## 7. Fee Statementing and Collection Receipt Ledger Isolation

### Fixture for LED tests

Use patient B and these documents:

```text
Fee Statement A: 1,200; clinician A line 700, clinician B line 500
Collection R1: 400; Cash
Collection R2: 600; UPI
```

### LED-001 [Phase 1] Fee Statement only

1. Issue Fee Statement A with no collection_receipt.
2. Open Fee Statement Register, Daily Fee Statement Amount, Due Fee Statements, Daily Collection Amount, unsettled register.

Expected:

```text
Production = 1,200
Due = 1,200
Collection = 0
Applied = 0
Unsettled = 0
```

Journal: debit receivable 1,200; credit revenue/tax/round-off components 1,200.

### LED-002 [Phase 2] Unapplied single-mode collections

1. Open `Record Collection` and verify `Amount`, one `Collection Method`, `Collection Reference`, `Collection Date`, `Cheque/Ref #`, Notes, Save, and Cancel.
2. Verify the form contains no Fee Statement selector, split-tender lines, Apply, or Settle command.
3. Create Collection R1 for Cash 400 and Collection R2 for UPI 600 without selecting a Fee Statement.
4. Open all collection, applied-collection, due, and unsettled reports.

Expected:

```text
Gross collection = 1,000
Cash collection = 400
UPI collection = 600
Applied = 0
Fee Statement due = 1,200
Collections unapplied = 1,000
Unsettled register = 1,000
```

Journal: debit cash/UPI clearing 1,000; credit patient advance 1,000. No receivable settlement.

### LED-003 [Phase 2] Partial application with collection-mode isolation

1. Through the configured settlement service or authorized test harness, apply all 400 from R1 and 300 from R2 to Fee Statement A.
2. Open clinician-wise and collection-mode applied reports.

Expected:

```text
Collection remains 1,000
Applied = 700
Due = 500
Unapplied/unsettled = R2 balance 300 UPI
Applied by mode = Cash 400 + UPI 300 = 700
```

Clinician allocation totals equal 700 and follow saved fee statement-line distribution. No report may show 700 under both Cash and UPI.

### LED-004 [Phase 2] Full allocation

- Apply the remaining 300 UPI from R2 to Fee Statement A.

Expected: collection 1,000; applied 1,000; due 200; unsettled 0; R1 and R2 are fully applied.

### LED-005 [Phase 2] Application reversal

- Reverse the second 300 application with reason.

Expected: collection stays 1,000; applied returns to 700; due returns to 500; R2 unsettled returns to 300 UPI; reversal audit/journal/clinician share all reconcile.

### LED-006 [Phase 2] Concurrent over-application

- From two sessions attempt to apply the same remaining 300 simultaneously.

Expected: one succeeds, one returns conflict/idempotent result; collection unapplied never negative; fee statement never over-applied.

### LED-007 [Phase 2] Later refund

- Refund the unapplied 300 UPI from R2 on a later date.

Expected:

```text
Original-day gross collection remains 1,000
Refund-day refund = 300
Period net collection = 700 when both dates included
Applied = 700
Due = 500
Unapplied = 0
```

Refund is numbered, linked to original collection/mode, permissioned, audited, and journaled.

### LED-008 [Phase 2] Void controls

- Attempt to void collection with active application.
- Attempt to void paid/part-paid fee_statement.
- Follow authorized reversal sequence.

Expected: direct void is blocked; after reversals, void preserves numbers/history and removes source from active totals without deletion.

### LED-009 [Phase 2] Multi-fee statement allocation

- Create Fee Statement B for 600 and Collection S for 800 using one collection method.
- Apply 500 to A and 300 to B.

Expected: fee allocations sum 800; each fee statement due changes independently; collection counted once; application drill-down shows both fee_statements.

### LED-010 [Phase 2] Discount, tax, and round-off

- Issue multi-line fee statement with line discount, header discount, taxable/non-taxable lines, GST, and round-off.

Expected: allocated discounts sum exactly; fee statement total equals lines/tax/round-off; printed fee statement, Fee Statement Register, GST extract, and ledger match to the cent.

### LED-011 [Phase 2] Credit note versus refund versus write-off

- Apply each operation separately to identical fee_statements.

Expected:

- Credit note changes receivable/production adjustment, not collection.
- Refund changes cash/net collection and collection receipt availability.
- Write-off changes due, not collection/fee allocation.
- Each has distinct report/audit/journal treatment.

### LED-012 [Phase 2] Last modified collection receipt report

- Create collection on Day 1; edit allowed notes/reference on Day 3.

Expected: daily collection remains Day 1; Last Modified Date wise Collection Receipts finds it on Day 3; audit shows both dates.

## 8. Report Reconciliation

### REP-001 [Phase 2] Collection equality

For one filter scope verify:

```text
Daily Collection Amount
= sum Date, Collection mode wise collection summary
= sum Patient wise collection summary
= sum User wise Collection summary
= sum Patient Collection Receipts pivot mode columns
```

Expected: gross totals equal exactly; refund/net columns reconcile separately.

### REP-002 [Phase 2] Applied-collection equality

```text
sum Lead Clinician wise fee allocations detail
= applied-collection summary
= Date, Clinician, Collection mode applied summary
= Collection mode wise Patient Collections
```

Expected: all equal active application total after filter/date rules; multiple single-mode collections do not multiply.

### REP-003 [Phase 2] Production equality

- Compare Fee Statement Register, Daily/Monthly Fee Statement Amount, clinician production, patient production, and service production for equivalent scope.

Expected: header reports count fee statement totals once; line reports sum line totals; grouping sums reconcile to common production base.

### REP-004 [Phase 2] Due and unsettled isolation

- Create four patients: due only, advance only, both due and advance, settled.
- Open Due Fee Statements and collections-not-settled analytics.

Expected: each register includes only its correct source; patient net balance may offset for search display but reports do not erase the separate due/advance documents.

### REP-005 [Phase 2] Date semantics

- Create fee statement, collection receipt, application, refund, and modification on five different dates.
- Run each report for one day at a time.

Expected: each appears only under its authoritative business date in `04`.

### REP-006 [Phase 2] Grid/export conformance

- Apply filters/sort and export spreadsheet/PDF/print.

Expected: same effective filters, row set, grand totals, date interpretation, and source-as-of; export action audited.

### REP-007 [Phase 2] Exact report catalog

- Compare every category and leaf label against document 04, including Patients, Care Bookings, Referrals, Clinic, Analytics, and Inventory.
- Confirm examples `All Patients with Unallocated Collections(Advance)`, `OPD Efficiency Analysis`, and `Stock Position - Concise with barcode` appear under the correct categories.

Expected: no report is silently renamed, flattened into another category, or omitted; a feature-flagged report is visibly recorded as such in the traceability matrix.

### REP-008 [Phase 2] Open Fee Exposure aging boundaries and as-of math

1. Freeze test As-of Date and create seven issued INR 1,000 Fee Statements whose age-start dates are exactly 0, 30, 31, 60, 61, 90, and 91 days earlier.
2. Apply INR 250 to the age-31 Fee Statement before As-of and reverse it after As-of.
3. Apply an INR 100 credit to the age-61 Fee Statement and an INR 50 write-off to the age-90 Fee Statement before As-of.
4. Create an unapplied INR 500 patient advance with no application.
5. Run Due Fee Statements/Open Fee Exposure as of the frozen date.

Expected:

```text
0-30  = 2,000  (days 0 and 30)
31-60 = 1,750  (day 31 due 750 + day 60 due 1,000)
61-90 = 1,850  (day 61 due 900 + day 90 due 950)
90+   = 1,000  (day 91 only)
Total Due = 6,600
```

The later reversal does not change the earlier As-of result; moving As-of after reversal restores INR 250 due. The INR 500 unapplied advance appears only in the advance/unsettled register and does not reduce Open Fee Exposure.

### REP-009 [Phase 2] Open Fee Exposure filters and Clinician Split

- Put the boundary Fee Statements in two clinics and two patient categories; change one patient's current category after Fee Statement issue.
- Give one Fee Statement two lines assigned to clinician A and clinician B.
- Test Clinic Branch, optional Statement Date Range, Snapshot/Current category basis, clinician A, clinician B, both clinicians, and Clinician Split.

Expected: unauthorized clinic never appears; Snapshot category remains stable; Current category follows the patient master; selecting both clinicians does not duplicate the multi-clinician Fee Statement; Clinician Split line totals sum exactly to the unsplit fee statement due; every Fee Statement belongs to one aging bucket.

### REP-010 [Phase 2] Collection Activity by Date pivot and cashier totals

On Day 1 create:

```text
Cashier A: Cash 100, UPI 200, Card 300, Net Banking 400
Cashier B: Cash 60, UPI 70
```

On Day 2 post a Card refund of INR 40 against Cashier A's original Collection. On Day 3 apply INR 500 of the collected money to Fee Statements without creating a new Collection. On Day 4 reverse the refund.

Expected for Day 1:

```text
Cash = 160
UPI = 270
Card = 300
Net Banking = 400
Other = 0
Gross Collection = 1,130
Cashier A Gross = 1,000
Cashier B Gross = 130
```

Expected for Day 2: Gross 0, Refund 40, Net -40. Expected for Days 1-3: Gross 1,130, Refund 40, Net 1,090. Day 4 has Refund -40 and Net +40; Days 1-4 return to Net 1,130 without erasing Day-2 history. The Day-3 application appears only under Fee Allocations; it does not change collection mode columns, cashier totals, or Day-1 gross.

### REP-011 [Phase 2] Collection filters, snapshots, and unassigned clinician

- Assign Cashier A's Collections to clinician A/Category Standard snapshots; leave Cashier B's Collections without encounter/clinician and change the patient's current category to Corporate after collection.
- Test clinic, date, Snapshot/Current category, clinician A, Include Unassigned, cashier, and collection-mode filters independently and in combination.

Expected: clinician A snapshot filter returns gross 1,000; including Unassigned adds Cashier B's 130; Snapshot and Current category behavior is explicit; every filtered row and footer reconcile to source collection receipt tenders.

### REP-012 [Phase 2] Source, projection, grid, and export conformance

- Run product-contract reports from source SQL, fresh projection, intentionally stale projection, grid, print, PDF, and spreadsheet.
- Compare query version, effective filters, category basis, aging boundary, source-as-of, detail rows, and totals.

Expected: fresh projection equals source SQL; stale projection falls back to source or reports unavailable; every renderer is identical to INR 0.00; no asynchronous export broadens clinic scope or uses a different query version.

## 9. System Configuration and Serial Controls

### SET-001 [Phase 3] System Configuration hierarchy

- Verify the complete tree and exact labels from `06`.
- Verify search/deep links do not flatten categories.
- Verify the top-level groups include Schedule, Dental Chart, Document Output, Note Assist, Dental Lab, and App Options as separate groups.
- Disable a master already used historically.

Expected: no new selection; historical documents still display snapshot/reference.

### SET-003 [Phase 1] Clinic/Clinician-Staff split-screen

1. At 1440x900 and 1024x768 open `System Configuration -> Practice and Workforce`.
2. Verify `Practice Identity` options occupy the left pane and `Workforce and Access` options occupy the right pane simultaneously.
3. Verify the panes are not tabs, cards, separate routes, an accordion, or a wizard; check the vertical divider and independent scroll/footer regions.
4. Make both panes dirty, save only Clinic Setting, then switch clinic.
5. Repeat below 1024 px.

Expected: both groups remain side-by-side at supported desktop widths; saving one does not submit/reset the other; dirty confirmation prevents loss; narrow layout stacks Clinic first and Clinician/Staff second without overlap.

### IAM-001 [Phase 1] Staff profile without login

1. As a user with `configuration.workforce.edit` but no security-user permissions, create a synthetic assistant profile and assign Central clinic.
2. Verify `Create Login` is hidden and direct `POST /api/security/users` is attempted manually.

Expected: staff and staff-clinic rows exist; no user, credential, session, role, or membership row exists; UI shows `No Login`; direct API call returns 403 and creates no partial data.

### IAM-002 [Phase 1] Create login and link to staff

1. As Security Admin select the staff row and choose `Create Login`.
2. Enter a unique Login Name, display name, email/mobile, temporary password plus confirmation, Must Change Password, Active status, Central/North clinic access, Central default, and Reception role.
3. Add an explicit deny for `analytics.financial.view`; save once and immediately retry the same request.
4. Sign in with the new credentials and change the temporary password.

Expected: exactly one user, credential, staff-user link, two clinic memberships, role assignments, and deny override are created. Password is Argon2id-hashed and absent from responses/audit/logs. Default clinic is Central; the staff link is unique; retry is idempotent.

### IAM-003 [Phase 1] Unique credentials and link constraints

- Attempt the same Login Name with different case.
- Attempt a duplicate organization email where unique-email policy is enabled.
- Try linking the staff profile to a second user and the user to a second staff profile.

Expected: every duplicate is rejected before commit with field-level feedback; no serial/credential/membership residue remains.

### IAM-004 [Phase 1] Granular role and override precedence

1. Give the test user a role granting patient view/edit, Fee Statement view/create, Collection create, operational reports, and report export.
2. Do not grant Fee Statement void, Collection refund, financial reports, patient merge, user administration, or backup.
3. Add explicit Allow for `analytics.financial.view`, verify access, then replace it with explicit Deny while a role grants the same permission.
4. Test each permission from the menu/button and by a manually constructed API call.

Expected: distinct View/Create/Edit/Execute/Print/Export flags behave independently. Explicit deny wins over allow and role grants; allow wins over absent role grant; default is deny. Menu visibility and API result agree.

### IAM-005 [Phase 1] Authorization middleware bypass resistance

- From receptionist session call patient edit, Fee Statement void, Collection refund, financial report, report export, role edit, user disable, backup, and another-clinic endpoints directly.
- Change route IDs, JSON clinic IDs, query filters, saved-report IDs, WebSocket clinic channel, and asynchronous export payloads.

Expected: authentication, active membership, effective permission, record clinic, state, and field-scope checks all run server-side. Requests return 401/403 or non-enumerating 404 as designed; no row/job/file/event is created; sensitive denial is audited.

### IAM-006 [Phase 1] Immediate revocation and account lifecycle

1. Keep the test user signed in on two sessions.
2. Remove a role, add a deny override, remove North membership, then Disable Login.
3. Retry an already-open action and WebSocket subscription after each change.
4. Unlock and reset password as authorized admin.

Expected: `authz_version` changes invalidate cached capabilities; removed actions fail immediately; North data disappears; disabling revokes both sessions; unlock/reset is audited; old password and reset token cannot be reused.

### IAM-007 [Phase 1] Delegation and last-admin safeguards

- As a limited user with role-management UI access, attempt to grant a permission or clinic outside their delegation ceiling.
- Attempt to disable, demote, unlink, or remove the final active Security Admin from the final administered clinic.

Expected: both UI and API block escalation/lockout atomically and explain the required corrective step without exposing sensitive policy data.

### IAM-008 [Phase 1] Delete semantics

- Grant draft-delete permissions and delete an unposted clinical note/document draft.
- Attempt hard delete of a completed service, issued Fee Statement, posted Collection, application, journal, and audit event, including as Security Admin.

Expected: permitted drafts are removed or tombstoned according to policy; posted records cannot be hard-deleted by any role and expose only archive/void/reversal/supersede actions with reason and audit.

### SER-001 [Phase 2] Concurrent document numbers

- Submit 20 patient/fee statement/collection receipt creations concurrently across two users in one clinic.

Expected: unique ordered allocations, no duplicate rendered number, failed transaction leaves at most a void/reserved gap according to policy, and no number is reused.

### SER-002 [Phase 2] Generation type and clinic isolation

- Verify exact rows `Patient Code`, `Collection Reference`, `Statement Reference`, `Lab Order No.`, `Exp. Voucher`, `Goods In`, and `Goods Out`.
- For Central/North configure different prefixes/start values and test `Manual Serial Number`, `Year based Serial`, and `Year Month based Serial`, crossing year/month boundaries in the test clock.

Expected: each clinic/generation period uses the correct series; old numbers remain unchanged; preview is not consumed. Do not infer a financial-year reset unless separately specified.

### SER-003 [Phase 2] Unsafe serial edit

- Attempt prefix/next-number change that collides with issued document.

Expected: blocked with collision detail; privileged non-colliding change is versioned/audited.

### SET-002 [Phase 3] Document Output conformance

- For Fee Statement, Collection Receipt, Clinical Summary, Medication Order, and Care Plan verify Page Size, Orientation, Logo placement, Barcode type/size/text/placement, Page Header, Footer, Margins, Spacings, Font, Options, Save, and Reset.
- Save a synthetic template, print a dummy document, then Reset.

Expected: saved layout is document-type and clinic scoped; historical print snapshot does not mutate; Reset restores the effective default.

## 10. Communications

### COM-001 [Phase 3] Welcome channel choices

- Register patients with SMS only, WhatsApp only, both, neither.

Expected: exact queued channel set; patient save unaffected by provider outage.

### COM-002 [Phase 3] Bulk suppression preview

- Build list containing valid, duplicate, invalid, marketing-opted-out, and transactional-only recipients.

Expected: preview counts and eligible recipient list are exact; final send requires explicit confirmation; suppressed recipients never reach provider.

### COM-003 [Phase 3] Webhook order/idempotency

- Replay duplicate and out-of-order submitted/sent/delivered/failed events.

Expected: one provider event row per ID; delivered does not regress; reports count messages, not events.

### COM-004 [Phase 3] Credit exhaustion

- Exhaust SMS credits during a batch.

Expected: remaining messages pause/retry with admin alert; no silent loss or duplicate send after recharge.

## 11. Practice Assets, Lab, and Inventory

### OFF-001 [Phase 3] Expense lifecycle

- Save/post expense, verify daily/head/supplier reports and ledger.
- Void with reason.

Expected: reversal appears; historical voucher retained; totals reconcile.

### INV-001 [Phase 3] Goods inward/outward

- Post inward 10 units, outward 3, reverse outward, then post outward 4.

Expected stock: 10 -> 7 -> 10 -> 6. Movement history remains append-only; In Stock and inventory reports agree.

### INV-002 [Phase 3] Negative stock and reorder

- Attempt excessive outward under block and override policies.
- Move on hand to reorder level.

Expected: policy/permission enforced; one open low-stock alert; no duplicate alerts per worker retry.

### LAB-001 [Phase 3] Lab dates and statement

- Create a job using `Ref No`, `Lab Work`, `Assigned To Lab`, `Teeth`, `Shade and Notes`, `Request Date`, `Requested By`, `Expected Date`, `Work Status`, `Work Step`, `Received Date`, `Received By`, and `Amount`.

Expected: each date-wise report uses correct date; pending clears on received; statement opening + charges - collections = closing.

## 12. Security, Audit, Reliability, and Performance

### SEC-001 [Phase 1] Clinic isolation

- Sign in as Central-only user and manipulate URL/API clinic IDs for North.

Expected: no list, report, file, patient, or financial data leaks; denial is audited where appropriate.

### SEC-002 [Phase 1] Permission matrix

- Execute IAM-004 and IAM-005 for receptionist, cashier, clinician, auditor, and admin against patient view/edit, medication order save (and sign only when extension-enabled), Fee Statement issue/void, Collection create/refund/application reverse, operational/financial report view/export, settings, user/role administration, backup, and merge.

Expected: every permission code is independently enforced by UI and server; a manually constructed request cannot gain access.

### AUD-001 [Phase 1] Immutable history

- Review patient edit, care booking change, encounter transitions, Fee Statement issue, Collection, application/reversal, refund, medication order save/sign when enabled, settings edit, stock posting, and report export.

Expected: actor, clinic, before/after or source/reversal, reason, and timestamp exist; no UI action hard-deletes source history.

### REL-001 [Phase 1] Outbox atomicity

- Force failure before transaction commit and after commit/before worker processing.

Expected: before commit, neither domain row nor outbox exists; after commit, both exist and job retries safely.

### REL-002 [Phase 1] Projection rebuild

- Corrupt only test cached balances/projections, run reconciliation/rebuild.

Expected: source documents remain unchanged; caches restore exactly; repair audited.

### PERF-001 [Phase 1] Reception workload

- Seed representative volume: 100k patients, 1m care_bookings/encounters, 1m fee statement/collection receipt rows, 50 chairs/resources across authorized clinics.
- Execute performance budgets in `05` with 20 concurrent reception users.

Expected: p95 targets pass, no duplicate serial/queue/application, and UI maintains stable dimensions.

## 13. Project DentOS Conformance Traceability Matrix

Maintain a living matrix with columns:

```text
Reference route/screen
Specified label/control
Reference precondition
Target route/control
Target database command/query
State before/after
Background jobs
DentOS evidence artifact ID
Target screenshot/video ID
UAT test IDs
Status: unspecified / specified / implemented / verified / exception-approved
```

Required rows include every navigation item, quick action, Schedule view/action, Patients filter/form field, all 13 patient workspace tabs, Clinical Queue filter/action/state, Messages tab/route, Practice Assets tab, System Configuration leaf, Clinic-left/Clinician-Staff-right element, staff/user field, permission code, authorization middleware decision, serial row/generation type, print setting, and report leaf listed in documents 01-06.

## 14. Severity and Exit Criteria

Severity 1:

- Data leakage, lost/duplicated financial entry, duplicate serial, over-application, unbalanced journal, unsafe medication order, unrecoverable corruption.

Severity 2:

- Wrong report total/date/mode/clinician, broken schedule-to-Clinical Queue handoff, hidden primary action, unusable dense layout, permission bypass, lost notification job.

Severity 3:

- Minor label/spacing/export presentation difference with no workflow or numeric impact.

Final exit:

- 100% Critical and High tests pass.
- 100% financial reconciliation equalities pass to INR 0.00 difference.
- Zero Severity 1/2 open defects.
- Visual screenshots approved at both required desktop viewports.
- Accessibility keyboard/focus checks pass.
- Backup restore test passes.
- All inferred clinical screens are either confirmed against authorized dummy data or recorded as explicit, release-blocking conformance gaps.

No team member may report "complete declared-scope coverage" while the traceability matrix contains `unspecified`, `unmatched`, or unapproved exception rows.

<!-- ZERO_SHORTCUT_EXPANSION -->

## 14A. Intent, Consultation, Bundle, and Conversion Acceptance

### INT-001 [Phase 3] Mandatory intent tier at patient creation

1. Open Register Patient with `patient.create` and `patient.intent_tier.edit`.
2. Complete every required identity field but leave Intent Tier empty; submit each of Save Profile, Save and Create Booking, and Save and Start Encounter.
3. Verify all three commands return field validation and create no `patients`, `patient_clinic_links`, or `patient_intent_tier_events` row.
4. Select `1 Star Do Not Treat`, choose a reason code permitted for the 1 Star tier, enter a nonblank suitability note, and save.
5. Verify `patients.intent_tier = 'one_star_do_not_treat'`, the reason and supplied note are persisted, assessor/time are populated, and exactly one immutable event records old tier null and new tier 1 Star.
6. Repeat with 2 Star and 3 Star fixtures and verify their exact enum values, reason compatibility, and event history.

Expected: every patient has one accountable current intent tier; the tier is not used to deny emergency or clinically necessary care.

### INT-002 [Phase 3] Intent reassessment and historical snapshot isolation

1. Create a patient as 2 Star, finalize a consultation, and record its intent-tier snapshot.
2. Change the patient to 3 Star with a permitted reason, a valid optional note, actor, clinic-local timestamp, and matching row version.
3. Verify the patient current tier is 3 Star and one event records 2 Star to 3 Star without modifying the prior event.
4. Verify the finalized consultation remains 2 Star in historical reports while a later consultation snapshots 3 Star.
5. Attempt same-tier change, stale-row change, incompatible reason, a note longer than 1000 characters, direct event update, and direct event delete.

Expected: invalid mutations fail; current-cohort screens show 3 Star; dated conversion reports preserve each consultation's tier snapshot.

### CAS-001 [Phase 3] Dual-clinician consultation attribution

1. Create an open case from an encounter and choose one active Primary Consult Doctor and a different active Secondary Review Doctor from the same clinic.
2. Enter Consultation Objective, Consultation Summary, all required decision fields, and a valid Primary treatment bundle.
3. Finalize the consultation and inspect `case_consultations`, `clinical_cases`, `clinical_case_state_events`, audit, and outbox rows.
4. Verify primary and secondary IDs are stored independently and the patient's Lead Clinician is not copied into either field unless explicitly selected.
5. Attempt to use the same doctor in both roles, an inactive doctor, a doctor outside the clinic, a missing primary doctor, and a missing secondary doctor.

Expected: valid attribution is immutable after finalization; every invalid combination fails atomically without a partial consultation or state event.

### CAS-002 [Phase 3] Treatment bundle hierarchy and service lineage

1. Add three care-plan services from distinct service domains to an open clinical case.
2. Create one Primary, one Secondary, and one Tertiary bundle, each with an explicit label, exact advised/accepted lifecycle state, display order, linked care-plan services, service-domain snapshots, and proposed amounts.
3. Verify one active bundle per tier, ordered service rows, and exact bundle totals.
4. Attempt a duplicate active Primary bundle, a service owned by another patient, a service outside the case care plan, a negative amount, and a proposed amount above the linked care-plan service value.
5. Finalize the consultation and attempt to rewrite its service-domain snapshots or linked care-plan-service identities.

Expected: invalid graph writes fail; immutable snapshots remain reportable after master-data renaming; Primary, Secondary, and Tertiary remain distinct.

### CAS-003 [Phase 3] Explicit case execution states

1. Finalize four synthetic cases respectively as `Not Started`, `Minor Issue & Treated Same Day`, `No Treatment Needed`, and `Treatment Started` with evidence permitted by document 02.
2. Verify the exact enum value, state reason, source type, actor, source encounter, changed time, and one state-event row for each case.
3. Try to set Minor Issue & Treated Same Day without same-encounter completed care delivery.
4. Try to set Treatment Started without same-encounter delivery or a qualifying future-encounter applied payment.
5. Correct a finalized state using `clinical_case.correct_state` with controlled reason and mandatory note.

Expected: unsupported states fail; correction appends history and preserves the original event.

### PAY-001 [Phase 3] Qualifying future applied payment starts a case

1. Finalize a case as Not Started with an advised Primary bundle linked to care-plan service `S1` in encounter `E1`.
2. At later encounter `E2`, issue a Fee Statement line linked by `fee_statement_lines.care_plan_service_id = S1`.
3. Record a Collection Receipt, create an active positive Fee Allocation, and create a positive allocation fee-line split for that exact line.
4. Commit the allocation while two application workers and one API retry are active.
5. Verify the case advances once to Treatment Started, stores the qualifying allocation and future encounter, appends exactly one state event, emits one state-change outbox event, and leaves all collection/allocation/journal amounts unchanged.

Expected: the database trigger is authoritative and idempotent; the EOD reconciler later reports no additional mutation.

### PAY-002 [Phase 3] Unapplied collection does not start a case

1. Finalize a 3 Star case as Not Started with an advised plan service.
2. Record a positive Collection Receipt at a future encounter but create no Fee Allocation.
3. Run event consumers, report projection refresh, and the EOD case progression reconciler.
4. Verify `clinical_cases.execution_state` remains Not Started and no automatic state event references the receipt.
5. Verify the receipt appears in the unsettled collections register and does not appear as an applied value in conversion reports.

Expected: money collected and revenue settled against an advised fee are never conflated.

### PAY-003 [Phase 3] Wrong-line and wrong-case allocations do not start a case

1. Create Not Started case `C1` with advised service `S1` and another case `C2` or unrelated service `S2` for the same patient.
2. At a later encounter, apply money only to a Fee Statement line linked to `S2`.
3. Run the transactional trigger and EOD reconciliation.
4. Verify `C1` remains Not Started, `C2` follows only its own evidence, and no cross-case state event exists.
5. Repeat with another patient and another clinic using deliberately similar service codes.

Expected: UUID lineage and patient/clinic constraints prevent textual-code or same-patient false positives.

### PAY-004 [Phase 3] Same-encounter, zero, void, and reversed evidence exclusions

1. For separate Not Started cases, create: an allocation tied to the initial consultation encounter; a zero-value split; a Fee Statement in draft; a void Fee Statement; a reversed allocation; and a positive allocation with no bundle-service link.
2. Invoke immediate progression and EOD reconciliation for every fixture.
3. Verify all cases remain Not Started and the excluded evidence is visible only in its correct financial audit/history location.
4. Confirm the worker watermark advances past inspected rows without inventing a state transition.

Expected: every proof condition is conjunctive; no excluded transaction qualifies.

### PAY-005 [Phase 3] Reversal after legitimate progression

1. Start a Not Started case through the valid PAY-001 sequence.
2. Reverse the qualifying Fee Allocation using the permitted financial workflow.
3. Verify due, applied, unsettled, ledger, and reversal reports change according to document 03.
4. Verify the clinical case does not silently regress to Not Started and the original progression event remains immutable.
5. Verify a visible evidence-review indicator is raised; use `clinical_case.correct_state` only when an authorized clinician determines a correction is clinically justified.

Expected: accounting reversal and clinical-history correction are separate authorized actions.

### REP-013 [Phase 2] Monthly Total Category Consultations

1. Seed finalized consultations across two clinics, two clinic-local months, and three service domains; include repeated services in one domain within one case.
2. Run the report with explicit clinic, month range, domain, and consultation-status filters.
3. Verify one case contributes once per distinct domain, repeated same-domain services do not multiply it, and clinic-local month boundaries are honored.
4. Reconcile Total Consultations, Distinct Patients, and Distinct Cases to the full SQL in document 04.
5. Verify grid, print, and export have identical row sets and totals.

Expected: all reconciliations equal zero difference.

### REP-014 [Phase 2] High-Intent Pipeline Bottleneck

1. Seed each intent tier across every execution state, with consultation dates spanning the As-of Date.
2. Run the report without optional doctor/domain/value filters.
3. Verify only 3 Star plus Not Started cases at or before As-of Date appear.
4. Apply primary doctor, secondary doctor, service-domain, and minimum-proposed-value filters separately and jointly.
5. Verify Days Pending uses whole clinic-local days and drill-down opens the exact source case.

Expected: the fixed tier/state predicates cannot be weakened by browser parameters.

### REP-015 [Phase 2] Doctor-Wise Clinical Conversion Ratios

1. Seed finalized initial consultations for two Primary Consult Doctors across a clinic-local date range, including every execution state and one doctor with zero consultations.
2. Run with all primary doctors, then each Primary Consult Doctor, treatment category, current/case-snapshot tier basis, and intent-tier filter.
3. Verify the denominator counts distinct finalized initial consultations assigned to each immutable Primary Consult Doctor.
4. Recalculate `Conversion Percentage = Treatment Started Cases / Total Initial Consultations * 100`; verify Minor Issue & Treated Same Day remains outside the numerator.
5. Verify a zero denominator renders a blank percentage, every numerator is a distinct case count, and As-of At excludes a treatment start after the selected cut-off.

Expected: primary and secondary totals reconcile independently to the source fixture.

### REP-016 [Phase 2] High-Value Category Conversions

1. Mark Endodontics, Implantology, and Orthodontics as high value with explicit floors; leave one control domain unmarked.
2. Seed bundle services for each domain plus Treatment Started and non-started cases under distinct Primary and Secondary doctors.
3. Run each selected month by Primary attribution and Secondary attribution.
4. Verify the unmarked domain is absent and each case contributes once per high-value domain and selected doctor role.
5. Verify Advised Value and Converted Advised Value trace to immutable bundle-service snapshots without adding Primary and Secondary role rows as unique clinic totals.

Expected: clinician, month, domain, production, applied value, and conversion fields reconcile to document 04 SQL.

### REP-017 [Phase 2] Cross-Tier Matrix Generator

1. Seed each of the three intent tiers for every execution state across single-category and multi-category cases.
2. Run every nonempty selection of Group Dimensions from Treatment Category, Intent Tier, and Execution Status with doctor/domain/state filters.
3. Verify omitted dimensions render `All` and selected dimensions display their exact group value.
4. Verify Case Count, Total Advised Value, Average Advised Value, Treatment Started Count, and Treatment Started Percentage for every generated row.
5. Switch Intent Tier Basis between Current and Case Snapshot after changing patient current tiers and rerun.

Expected: historical matrix cells do not change after current-tier reassessment.

### REP-018 [Phase 2] Pending Priority Treatment Registers

1. Seed Not Started and Treatment Started cases containing Primary, Secondary, and Tertiary bundles with Presented, Accepted, Declined, and Superseded states.
2. Select a month with Target Start basis; run the Primary register and verify only Primary bundles meeting the selected execution/bundle filters appear.
3. Select the same month with Advised basis; run the Secondary register and verify only Secondary bundles meeting the selected filters appear.
4. Verify Tertiary bundles appear in case drill-down but never in either priority register.
5. Reconcile Target or Advised Date, Pending Services, Pending Service Count, Pending Advised Value, primary doctor, secondary doctor, treatment categories, intent tier, and execution state to document 04 SQL.

Expected: the two registers are isolated, stable under paging, and identical across grid, print, and export.

### SEC-003 [Phase 1] New permission enforcement

1. Create a role with `patient.intent_tier.view` only and verify tier display/history are readable while change commands return 403.
2. Grant `patient.intent_tier.edit`; verify authorized change succeeds and delegation ceiling is enforced.
3. Exercise `clinical_case.view`, `clinical_case.create`, `clinical_case.edit_state`, `clinical_case.correct_state`, `case_consultation.finalize`, and `treatment_bundle.manage` independently using hidden controls, direct URLs, crafted API calls, stale capability caches, and asynchronous retries.
4. Exercise `analytics.conversion.view` against all six query routes, print endpoints, export endpoints, saved export IDs, and download links.
5. Apply an explicit Deny over a role Allow and repeat every relevant operation.

Expected: server middleware and domain guards deny every unauthorized route without leaking rows, totals, source identifiers, or file artifacts.

## 15. Atomic Control UAT

### CTL-001 [Phase 1] Global shell / Dashboard

1. Sign in with a synthetic user whose active-clinic capabilities include `session active`; open `Global shell`.
2. Locate the `navigation link` labelled `Dashboard` at `top navigation position 1`; measure its declared width `route width 84 px`.
3. Verify the placeholder or tooltip is `none` and the input contract is `exact label`.
4. Traverse to the control by keyboard, verify a visible focus indicator, then operate it with its semantic activation key.
5. Repeat with pointer input and verify the resulting action is exactly: open /dashboard.
6. Verify active clinic, operational date, selected patient, selected encounter, current draft revision, selected row, filters, and scroll position remain coherent.
7. Remove `session active`, refresh capabilities, revisit the deep link, and submit the equivalent API request.
Expected: label, control type, placeholder, coordinate, width, validation, authorized action, denied state, HTTP result, audit result, and focus restoration equal the control registry; denial creates no domain row or outbox event.

### CTL-002 [Phase 1] Global shell / Clinical Queue

1. Sign in with a synthetic user whose active-clinic capabilities include `queue.view`; open `Global shell`.
2. Locate the `navigation link` labelled `Clinical Queue` at `top navigation position 2`; measure its declared width `route width 112 px`.

## 16. Atomic Permission UAT

### PERM-001 [Phase 1] patient.view

1. Open `System Configuration`, select the right-side `Workforce and Access` pane, and open the target account in `Permission Matrix`.
2. Locate module `patient_registry`, resource `patient`, action `view`, and permission code `patient.view`.
3. Verify the matrix control is `three-state matrix cell` and the inherited presentation is: role result shown read-only.
4. Set the override to `Allow`, save access policies, refresh the capability token, and verify: explicit override allows within delegation ceiling.
5. Exercise the matching visible control, direct API route, deep link, asynchronous export or worker command when applicable, and WebSocket or event subscription when applicable.
6. Set the override to `Deny`, save, refresh capabilities, and verify: explicit override denies and wins over all grants.
7. Repeat the direct request with a stale browser tab and a previously issued capability response; require HTTP 403 without resource enumeration or mutation.
8. Verify the security audit contains actor, target user, clinic, permission code, old effective state, new effective state, reason when sensitive, timestamp, and correlation ID without credentials or patient secrets.
Expected: explicit Deny wins over Allow and role grant; explicit Allow remains within the delegation ceiling; Inherit resolves only from current active roles; disabling the account invalidates the resulting capability.

2. Locate module `patient_registry`, resource `patient`, action `export`, and permission code `patient.export`.
3. Verify the matrix control is `three-state matrix cell` and the inherited presentation is: role result shown read-only.
4. Set the override to `Allow`, save access policies, refresh the capability token, and verify: explicit override allows within delegation ceiling.
5. Exercise the matching visible control, direct API route, deep link, asynchronous export or worker command when applicable, and WebSocket or event subscription when applicable.
6. Set the override to `Deny`, save, refresh capabilities, and verify: explicit override denies and wins over all grants.
7. Repeat the direct request with a stale browser tab and a previously issued capability response; require HTTP 403 without resource enumeration or mutation.
8. Verify the security audit contains actor, target user, clinic, permission code, old effective state, new effective state, reason when sensitive, timestamp, and correlation ID without credentials or patient secrets.
Expected: explicit Deny wins over Allow and role grant; explicit Allow remains within the delegation ceiling; Inherit resolves only from current active roles; disabling the account invalidates the resulting capability.

Expected: explicit Deny wins over Allow and role grant; explicit Allow remains within the delegation ceiling; Inherit resolves only from current active roles; disabling the account invalidates the resulting capability.

### PERM-083 [Phase 1] security.user.disable

3. Verify the matrix control is `three-state matrix cell` and the inherited presentation is: role result shown read-only.
4. Set the override to `Allow`, save access policies, refresh the capability token, and verify: explicit override allows within delegation ceiling.
5. Exercise the matching visible control, direct API route, deep link, asynchronous export or worker command when applicable, and WebSocket or event subscription when applicable.
